stimulus: Noticing that pond water teeming with microscopic life appeared to spontaneously develop maggots when left covered with cloth, seventeenth-century naturalists did not conclude that flies had laid eggs on the cloth; instead, many accepted the older view that life could arise from nonliving matter through a process called spontaneous generation. ______blank when Francesco Redi designed his famous sealed-jar experiment in 1668, he framed it as a direct test of that theory.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. "For this reason" logically signals that the reason Redi designed his sealed-jar experiment as a direct test of spontaneous generation was that this theory was already widely accepted, as mentioned in the previous sentence.

Choice A is incorrect because "that said" illogically signals that Redi's experiment was conducted despite the prevalence of the theory, when in fact the theory's prevalence is exactly why he targeted it. Choice B is incorrect because "in other words" illogically signals that Redi's experiment restates the previous information about spontaneous generation, when it actually follows from it. Choice C is incorrect because "otherwise" illogically signals an alternative scenario, but Redi's experiment was a direct response to the established view, not an alternative to it.
